title: Dental Care for Veterans Act
plain_summary: The Dental Care for Veterans Act would let all veterans who get health care from the VA also get dental care from the VA. Right now, most veterans can only get VA dental care if they have tooth problems caused by their military service or meet other special rules. The new law would change this over four years, with different groups of veterans getting dental care at different times based on things like their disabilities or how much money they make. This means many more veterans would be able to get their teeth fixed through the VA instead of having to pay for it somewhere else.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Dental Care for Veterans Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Requirement That Secretary of Veterans Affairs Furnish Dental Care in the Same Manner as Any Other Medical Service
sections.text: This section changes how the Department of Veterans Affairs provides dental care to veterans. Currently, dental care has special rules that are different from other medical care. This law removes those special rules so that dental care will be treated just like any other medical service that veterans receive. The changes will happen in phases over four years. Veterans who already get dental care from the VA will continue getting it right away when this law starts. Other veterans will become eligible for dental care based on their priority group. Veterans in higher priority groups will get dental care first, starting one year after the law begins. Veterans in lower priority groups will get dental care later, with the last group getting it four years after the law starts. The law also makes technical changes to remove old sections of law that will no longer be needed and updates references to match the new system.
